index.name: Nifty Energy Index
index.description: Nifty Energy tracks 10 companies spanning entire energy value chain: upstream (ONGC exploration, Coal India mining), midstream (NTPC generation, Power Grid transmission, GAIL pipelines), downstream (Reliance O2C refining, IOC marketing), and new energy (Adani Green solar). India's energy demand growing 5% annually — fastest globally — underpinned by 1.4B population, rising per-capita consumption, and electrification. Structural tailwind: renewable energy transition targets 50% capacity by 2032 (currently 35%), requiring ₹10 Lakh Cr capex. Coal India monopoly ensures stable cash flows (dividend yield 6-8%) but faces long-term coal-demand decline. NTPC/Power Grid benefit from transmission upgrade capex (₹1 Lakh Cr+ CapEx over 10 years for 'Green Energy Corridors' to evacuate Rajasthan/Gujarat solar). Reliance's ₹75,000 Cr new energy push creates optionality play. Oil price correlation: when Brent touches $100/barrel, Reliance O2C petrochemicals margins compress; when $70, they expand. Currency headwind: when rupee weakens, oil import costs rise but refinery margins improve (net neutral).
